_description: Dialogue quality evaluation results for the Base Llama 3.1 8B model (meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B, FP16) measured on the dissertation test system. Covers four conversational-quality dimensions: retention accuracy, dialogue coherence & consistency, frequency of forgotten information, and hallucination rate. All evaluations use a greedy-decoded base model without instruction tuning or RLHF.
_note: The base model is a text-completion engine; it has no explicit memory mechanism beyond the tokens in its context window. 'Retention' is therefore a measure of in-context attention and recall, not any external memory store.

system_info.decoding_strategy: greedy (temperature=0, do_sample=False) for consistency and reproducibility

methodology.overview: Four independent evaluation protocols were run against the base FP16 model. Each protocol was designed to isolate a specific failure mode of a base (non-instruction-tuned) language model in multi-turn conversational settings. Greedy decoding was used throughout to ensure reproducibility.

retention_accuracy.description: Measures how reliably the model can retrieve a specific fact that was injected into the conversation context N turns earlier, when directly probed for it. A 'turn' is one user message plus one model completion.
protocol: 1. Construct a dialogue context in which Turn 1 contains a specific, unambiguous fact (e.g. 'The password to the safe is 7391' or 'Alice's favourite colour is cerulean').
2. Insert N filler turns of neutral conversation to separate the probe from the fact.
3. At Turn N+2, issue a direct probe: 'What is the password to the safe?' / 'What is Alice's favourite colour?'.
4. Mark the response as CORRECT if and only if the exact fact appears verbatim or paraphrased unambiguously in the model's reply.
5. Repeat across 25 unique fact/probe pairs per context-length condition.

context_length_conditions.short: 3–5 filler turns (approx. 150–250 context tokens after the seed fact)
context_length_conditions.medium: 10–15 filler turns (approx. 700–900 context tokens after the seed fact)
context_length_conditions.long: 25–30 filler turns (approx. 1 800–2 200 context tokens after the seed fact)

retention_accuracy.scoring: Accuracy = correct retrievals / total probes (binary). 95% confidence intervals computed using the Wilson score interval.

dialogue_coherence.description: Measures whether successive model responses are topically and logically consistent with the evolving dialogue thread. An incoherent response introduces non-sequiturs, abruptly changes topic, or ignores a question asked by the prior turn.
protocol: 1. Seed 20 multi-turn dialogue transcripts (10–20 turns each) drawn from three topic categories: technical Q&A, narrative story-telling, and factual question-answering.
2. For each model response, two independent annotators rated coherence on a 5-point Likert scale (1 = completely incoherent / off-topic; 5 = fully coherent, on-topic, responsive).
3. Inter-annotator agreement measured with Cohen's κ; disputes resolved by discussion.
4. A response is classified as 'coherent' if its mean rating ≥ 3.

forgotten_information_rate.description: Measures how often key contextual facts, established earlier in a dialogue, are absent (forgotten) from a later response where their inclusion would be expected. Distinct from retention accuracy in that it covers implicit/indirect omissions, not just direct probes.
protocol: 1. Construct 30 dialogues in which each Turn 1 establishes 3 key facts (names, preferences, constraints).
2. Design Turn N (N ∈ {5, 10, 20}) to require implicit use of all 3 facts in a sensible response.
3. Rate each of the 3 facts as Present (explicitly referenced), Absent (completely missing), or Implicit (reasonably inferable from response).

hallucination_rate.description: Measures how frequently the model asserts factually incorrect information (hallucinations) during multi-turn dialogue. Two sub-types are tracked: factual hallucinations (wrong real-world facts) and context hallucinations (contradicts facts stated earlier in the same conversation).
protocol: 1. Seed 40 dialogues with prompts requiring specific factual answers (historical dates, scientific constants, code-execution results).
2. Ground-truth answers were pre-verified from authoritative sources.
3. Each model assertion was rated: CORRECT, HALLUCINATED (factually wrong), or N/A (subjective / not checkable).
5. Context hallucinations were additionally tracked: model contradicts a fact it stated itself ≤ 5 turns earlier.

retention_accuracy.summary: The base model retains injected context-window facts with high fidelity at short distances, but accuracy decays as conversation length grows and the seed fact is pushed further back in the KV-cache.
retention_accuracy.notes: Accuracy drop of ~20 pp from short to long context is consistent with attention-sink phenomena documented in long-context LLM evaluations (Xiao et al., 2023). The base model has no dedicated memory module; all retention is purely in-context.

dialogue_coherence.summary: Most responses are rated coherent, but the base model (without instruction tuning) occasionally drifts from the dialogue topic or answers a different question than the one asked.

consistency_across_turns.description: Rate at which the model's responses are logically consistent with its own prior statements within the same dialogue.
consistency_across_turns.notes: Self-contradictions most common in factual Q&A category, where the model asserts different 'facts' in different turns when the same topic recurs.

dialogue_coherence.notes: The base model performs best on structured technical Q&A (mean 3.91) and worst on factual Q&A (3.41), where it sometimes provides contradictory facts in successive turns. This is consistent with the low TruthfulQA score (44.7%) already documented in the NLP benchmark results.

forgotten_information_rate.summary: Omission of earlier-established context increases sharply with dialogue length. The model reliably incorporates recent facts but shows growing negligence for facts established 20+ turns prior.

turn_5.label: Probed at Turn 5 (fact established at Turn 1)

turn_10.label: Probed at Turn 10 (fact established at Turn 1)

turn_20.label: Probed at Turn 20 (fact established at Turn 1)

forgotten_information_rate.notes: The forgotten-information rate roughly doubles every 10 additional turns. This directly reflects the base model's inability to explicitly summarise or externally store context; only the raw token stream is available. At 20 turns the seed fact is typically > 1 500 tokens from the current position, and attention weight allocated to it decreases accordingly.

hallucination_rate.summary: The base model hallucinated verifiable facts at a significant rate, consistent with the expectation for a non-RLHF-aligned base model. Two hallucination sub-types were tracked.

factual_hallucinations.description: Model asserts a fact that is objectively incorrect when compared to ground-truth sources.
factual_hallucinations.notes: Cross-reference with TruthfulQA MC2 score of 44.7%; a ~55% 'wrong' rate on TruthfulQA provides a comparable upper bound.

context_hallucinations.description: Model contradicts a fact it stated itself within the last 5 turns of the same dialogue.
context_hallucinations.notes: Context hallucinations often involve numeric data (dates, quantities) and proper nouns. The model re-generates plausible-sounding but inconsistent values.

combined.note: The two sub-types are measured against different denominators and should not be directly averaged.

hallucination_rate.notes: Hallucination rates would be expected to decline substantially with instruction fine-tuning and RLHF (alignment tax trades lower creativity for higher factual reliability). These results establish the base-model ceiling for future comparison.

key_findings: Retention accuracy is strong at short distances (92% at 3–5 turns) but degrades to 72% at 25–30 turns, demonstrating the attention-based recall limit of the unaugmented context window.
Dialogue coherence averages 3.72/5; 77% of responses are rated coherent. Technical Q&A is the strongest category (82.5% coherent); factual Q&A the weakest (68.1%).
Self-consistency rate is 88.1%, meaning ~12% of turns contradict an earlier statement in the same conversation — most pronounced in factual Q&A dialogues.
Forgotten-information rate grows from 6.7% at Turn 5 to 33.3% at Turn 20, roughly doubling every 10 turns — a direct consequence of diminishing attention to distant tokens.

Factual hallucination rate of 28.6% is substantial and consistent with the TruthfulQA score of 44.7% already documented. Context hallucinations (model contradicts itself) occur in 11.9% of opportunities.

All four metrics confirm the well-documented limitations of base (pre-RLHF) models in conversational use and provide a concrete quantitative baseline for future instruction-tuned and RLHF-aligned variants.
references: Dubey, A. et al. (2024). The Llama 3 Herd of Models. Meta AI. arXiv:2407.21783.
Lin, S., Hilton, J., & Evans, O. (2021). TruthfulQA: Measuring How Models Mimic Human Falsehoods. arXiv:2109.07958.
Xiao, G. et al. (2023). Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks. arXiv:2309.17453.
Landis, J. R. & Koch, G. G. (1977). The Measurement of Observer Agreement for Categorical Data. Biometrics, 33(1), 159–174.
